title: Add riscv64 cross-compilation to CI
body:
Buildah builds and runs on riscv64 with no source changes needed.

```
$ ./bin/buildah version
Version:         1.43.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.25.5
OS/Arch:         linux/riscv64
BuildPlatform:   linux/riscv64
```

Built on BananaPi F3 (SpacemiT K1, rv64gc, 8 cores @ 1.6 GHz) in 5m14s.

Since Podman already has a riscv64 cross-compilation PR accepted in Cirrus CI (containers/podman#28333, approved by @Luap99), it would make sense to add the same for Buildah to keep the containers toolchain consistent.

Options:
1. Add `GOARCH=riscv64` cross-build to Cirrus CI (like Podman)
2. Add native riscv64 build via RISE runners - free native riscv64 GitHub Actions runners from the [RISE Project](https://riseproject.dev/). To enable: install the [RISE runners GitHub App](https://github.com/apps/rise-risc-v-runners). Already used by numpy, Apache Arrow, llama.cpp, and others.

Happy to submit a PR with either approach.

comments.body: Is this more than what the "Cross Compile" task does now?  I see it checking for compile on Linux and FreeBSD, for example, at https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6625474320793600?logs=main#L28 and https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6625474320793600?logs=main#L50

The current setup doesn't verify that the native libraries are there, and after a peek at https://github.com/gounthar/buildah/pull/1/changes I see that it does, which is nice (and I've bookmarked the github app).
Catching up on the conversation in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28329, it does sound like that's more than we want to get into, though.

Makes sense. The cross-compile coverage is good enough for catching Go-level regressions, and distro packaging handles the rest. Closing this.

Good to know the RISE runners app is on your radar if native builds ever become useful down the line.
